od_description: Batamaloo (main Srinagar terminal) ↔ Budgam / Badgam (Budgam district HQ) via Peerbagh.
stops_verdict: Batamaloo end well-corroborated (367 visits / 23 drivers = the terminal), but the corridor itself is thin (3 drivers).
narrative: Batamaloo ↔ Budgam via Peerbagh — a plausibly major Srinagar↔Budgam corridor (Batamaloo is the main minibus terminal, Budgam a district HQ). But NO candidate permit matches its O–D: all are Batamaloo-to-elsewhere (Arath, Khrew, Drussu) or Parimpora–Humhama, at just 0.22 overlap. THIN support (9 runs / 3 drivers / 6 days). Notable-if-confirmed: the Srinagar↔Budgam corridor may be under-covered by the permit plan — but too thin here to assert. Needs RTO check.
evidence_used: geocoded endpoints: Batamaloo (A, main terminal), Budgam/Badgam (B, district HQ), Peerbagh (mid)
no candidate matches O–D (all 0.22 overlap, wrong destinations)
data_quality_flags: potential under-covered corridor (Srinagar↔Budgam) but not asserted on 3 drivers
implied_headway 320 min is a partial-adoption artifact
